commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=bad3f65587a1eb36f1012bbb812166c5cfbfe896)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds `authProviderId` fallback lookups for memory embedding provider resolution in memory-core and the Gateway, plus a memory-core regression test for resolving `google` to the Gemini adapter.

PR surface: Source +25, Tests +24. Total +49 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main looks up memory embedding providers by adapter id or configured API owner, while the Google memory adapter is `gemini` with `authProviderId: "google"`. I did not run the live Google API key/index rebuild flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is needed because accepting `authProviderId` as a `memorySearch.provider` alias changes the config/provider contract; after that decision, the repair should be centralized before merge.

target.title: fix(memory): resolve embedding provider by authProviderId fallback
target.body:
## Summary

- When a user configures `memorySearch.provider` to `"google"`, the embedding provider lookup fails with "Unknown memory embedding provider: google" because the registered adapter id is `"gemini"` while the config-level provider name is `"google"`.
- The gemini memory embedding adapter declares `authProviderId: "google"` (it uses the Google auth provider for API keys), but the lookup functions only searched by adapter `id`.
- This fix adds an `authProviderId`-based fallback in both:
  - `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.ts` (`getAdapter`)
  - `src/gateway/embeddings-http.ts` (`createConfiguredEmbeddingProvider`)
- When a direct `id` lookup misses, the resolver now scans registered adapters for a matching `authProviderId` before falling through to generic embedding providers or throwing.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.test.ts` → 8 passed (including new regression test)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/embeddings-http.test.ts` → 36 passed
- Production function verified via `node --import tsx` (see Real behavior proof below)

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix / [ ] Feature / [ ] Refactor / [ ] Docs / [ ] Security / [ ] Chore

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / [ ] Skills / [ ] Auth / [x] Memory / [ ] Integrations / [ ] API / [ ] UI/UX / [ ] CI

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Configuring `memorySearch.provider: "google"` causes "Unknown memory embedding provider: google" in `--index` and `--deep` operations because the registered gemini adapter id does not match the "google" provider name.

**Environment tested:** Node 24.x on Linux, vitest + `node --import tsx` production function call.

**Steps run after the patch:** Listed registered memory embedding providers from the production source, confirmed the gemini adapter has `authProviderId: "google"`, and verified that the authProviderId-based lookup finds it.

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
Gemini adapter found:
  id: gemini
  authProviderId: google
  autoSelectPriority: 30
Test: authProviderId is "google": true
Lookup by authProviderId "google": gemini
```

**Observed result:** The gemini adapter is correctly resolved when looking up by `authProviderId: "google"`. All existing tests pass with the fallback logic in place.

**Not tested:** Live Rocky Linux upgrade flow from 2026.5.26 to 2026.6.1 with real Google AI Studio API key and memory index rebuild.

Closes #90786
target.author: lilan0125
